seoTitle: How to Create a New Outlook Account for Free
metaDescription: Learn how to create a new Outlook account, pick a username, set a secure password, pass verification, and set up backup security like passkeys.
intro: Setting up a free professional email account shouldn't require wrestling with confusing sign-up flows. This guide walks through Microsoft's streamlined outlook.com registration process, from choosing your username to configuring backup security options and opening your new inbox.

steps.heading: Start the sign-up
steps.body: Navigate to outlook.com in your browser and click Create free account.

steps.heading: Choose your username
steps.body: Type your desired handle into the input field, keeping the suffix set to @outlook.com.

steps.heading: Create a secure password
steps.body: Enter a unique password that mixes letters, numbers, and characters, using the visibility toggle to double-check your spelling.

steps.heading: Provide profile details
steps.body: Enter your first name, last name, region, and date of birth on the next screen.

steps.heading: Complete verification
steps.body: Solve the anti-bot puzzle prompt to confirm you're a real person.

steps.heading: Set up backup security
steps.body: Add a modern passkey using your device's biometric scanner, or provide a backup phone number or email for recovery, then wait for your mailbox to load.

faq.question: Is creating an Outlook account free?
faq.answer: Yes, signing up gives you a free Outlook Web App inbox along with 5 GB of OneDrive storage and Microsoft 365 free web apps.

faq.question: What if my desired username is already taken?
faq.answer: You'll need to try a variation of your desired handle, since usernames must be unique across the @outlook.com domain.

faq.question: Do I need to add a backup phone number?
faq.answer: It's optional but strongly recommended for account recovery if you ever get locked out.

faq.question: What can I do with my new account besides email?
faq.answer: Your account integrates with OneDrive storage and Microsoft 365 free web apps for documents and spreadsheets.
